def: n. f. S. अट् to wander, कल to reckon. 1. Discernment; judgment; skill ( āṅk ). Ise kapṛe kī baṛī aṭkal hai . He is a good judge of cloth. 2. Guess; rough estimate; conjecture. Phr. To give a guess. aṭ'kal-bāz , adj. One who makes a good guess.

n. m. A wild guess; a random guess; a mere conjecture; guesswork. Aṭkal-pachchū, gair muqarrar . W. Prov. = Uṭakkar pache deṛh sau . E. Prov. All guess and no certainty. aṭkal pachchū, uṭakkarlais , adj. 1. Uncertain; not exact; conjectural; without thought or premeditation. 2. Nonsensical; unmeaning; absurd; foolish. Soch-kar kaho, utakkarlais jawāb na do . Think before you speak, and don't talk nonsense. aṭkal se , adv. By guess; approximately; at random. aṭ'kal pach' chū kī fātiha paṛhnā , v. a. 1. To repeat a certain portion of the Korān as a prayer for a deceased person whose name is unknown. 2. To make a pretence of reading the Korān.
adj. Conjectural; probable; approximate.
adj. Conjectural; probable; presumptive.
v.t. To guess, conjecture; to judge, think; to estimate, value, appraise; to make a rough estimate; to find out; to see through (one)

def: v. a. 1. To guess; to make a rough guess; conjecture. 2. To estimate; make a rough estimate; value; assess ( āṅknā ). 3. To find out; to see through one; to take one's measure; to make out.

r: aṭ'kan maṭ'kan, aṭ'kal maṭ'kal, E. aṭ'kan baṭ'kan
def: W. n. m. A child's game. Aṭkan batkan, dahī chaṭākan; Aglā jhūle baglā jhūle; Sāwan mās karelā phūle . Children's game. A pot of rice, lick up the tyre; See swinging here, and swinging there; In August rains Karelā flowers.

v. a. 1. To hinder; interrupt; stop; hold back; impede; retard; hamper; obstruct; thwart; cut off; prevent; bar; clog; check. Phr. To stand in the way of; stop the way. 1. Chaltī gāṛī meṅ roṛā aṭkānā . To put a spoke in the wheel. 2. Pānī ne aṭkā rakkhā hai . The rain detains (me). 2. To confine; keep back; restrain; intercept; detain. 1. Rupaye ne iskī jān aṭkā rakkhī hai . His money detains his soul. 2. Ustād ne ek-hī kitāb meṅ aṭkā rakkhā hai . The master has kept me back in just one book. 3. To couple; attach; append; brace; join together; bracket. Bail ko jūe meṅ aṭkā do . Yoke the oxen. 4. To chain; fasten; hook; rivet. Kāṅṭe se kāṅṭā aṭkā lo . 5. To pin; stitch; to put in a few stitches ( ṭāṅkā lagānā ). Ab to zarā aṭkā do, phir pakkā ho jāëgā . Just stitch it for the present, it will be sewn afterwards. 6. To lock; lock up. 7. To betroth; to join in wedlock; to marry. Ab apne chhoṭe beṭe ko bhī kahīṅ aṭkā do . Now marry your younger son also to somebody. 8. To retrench; put under stoppages; withhold; keep back. Mȳāṅ, kyoṅ aṭkā rakkhā hai? De dilā ke pāp kāṭo . Sir, why do you keep back (my money). Give it and wipe off your sins. 9. To suspend; postpone; put off. Is muqadme ko aṭkā rakkho . 10. To wear; put on; slip on. Ab to isī ko aṭkā lo, phir dūsrā pahin lenā . Put on this for the present, you can wear another next time. aṭkāo meṅ . adv. Under control, restraint, etc.
v.t. To obstruct, hinder, impede, interrupt, stop, bar, prevent, check, restrain, keep back, detain; to fasten, join, attach, unite (in marriage); to put on, wear
more: (caus. of اٿکنا) v. a. To stop, to prevent, to hinder, restrain, detain, coerce.
subs: v.t. To keep back, withhold, detain; to put off, postpone

def: adj. Playful, sportive, lively, gay, merry; frivolous, light, coquettish, wanton.

adj. S. अट्ट much, खेल to move to and fro. 1. Playful; sportive; airy; sprightly; gay; lively; merry; lightsome; blithe; winsome; jovial; jolly. 2. Light; skittish; coquettish; wanton. aṭ-khe'lī, aṭkhel-panā,—pan , n. 1. Playfulness; sportiveness; sprightliness. 2. Mirth; merriment; merry-making; gaiety. 3. Coquettishness; an affected or mincing gait; graceful motion. Būṭā se qad, aur is tere aṭkhel-pane par, Ho kyoṅ na dugānāh * tere qurbān, dugānā? Wom. Raṅgīn. An elfin small, and such your sportive grace, How not to you, my soul, devoted be? 4. Friskiness; skittishness; amorous glances or gestures. aṭhkhelyāṅ, aṭhkelyoṅ kī chāl , n. f. 1. Sport; play; romp; sportive mirth. Bachchoṅ kī aṭhkhelyāṅ jī lubhātī haiṅ . The sportive mirth of children captivates the heart. 2. An affected or coquettish gait; coquettish airs; a mincing step. Chāl aṭkhelyoṅ se chaltī āī. Díl ko pāoṅ ke nīche maltī āī . Shauq. With a coquettish, wanton grace she moved, Trampling beneath her feet all hearts, she moved. aṭhkhelyoṅ se chalnā , v. n. To walk coquettishly, as a wanton, etc. Bach-pan meṅ sab aṭhkhelyoṅ se chalte haiṅ .
s.f. Playful, sportive, &c. girl or woman.

S. अड्ड join, सद् adhere; A jingling reduplication, n. f. 1. Knot; alliance; league; confederacy (sāzish) . 2. Attachment; friendship; affection (āshnāī) . Is ranḍī bhaṛve meṅ muddat se atsaṭ hai . There has been a long-standing liason between this harlot and the pimp. 3. Work; business; stroke of policy. Ghar kī aṭsaṭ meṅ, sārā din batīt hotā hai . Wom. The whole day goes in household work. 4. Skill; cleverness; head piece; ingenuity; shrewdness; hocus pocus (Slang); dexterity; adroitness; tact; knowledge of the world. Aṭsaṭ se chār paise kamā letā hai . By his adroitness he contrives to earn four pice a day. 5. Anxiety; care; solicitude. Rahe mudām sarokār aish se us ko! Na hone pāve kisī taur kī use aṭsaṭ . Inshā. May pleasure always with him be, Nor care t'assail him suffered be. aṭsaṭ ho'nā,—laṛ'nā , v. a. 1. To be linked to, entangled, etc. 2. To form a league, or confederacy; to enter into an alliance; to be combined. Sāre amle kī aṭsaṭ laṛī huī hai . All the ministerial officers are in league. 3. To fall in love; to be hooked (Slang) ; to be hand and glove with. Is se us se aṭ saṭ ho gaī . These two are entangled with one another.

s.f. Close union, alliance, league, confederacy; intimacy, close attachment, liaison, entanglement, amour; necessary or inseparable work or business (of a house); care, solicitude; shrewdness, astuteness; dexterity, skill, ingenuity, adroitness, tact
more: adj. (dakh.) Sixty eight: see اٿهسٿهہ aṭhsaṭh.
subs: (-se), To be attached (to); to be entangled (with); to be in league (with), &c.

An-tule bojh tale baiṭhā, sāṅchī sāṅchī bol . Rus. Now mind, you are sitting below an untold weight (the roof); so you had better speak the truth.
more: adj. S. चर्, चल, H. टल to move. 1. Fixed; immoveable; unmoved; rooted; fast; stationary; unable to stir. Phr. At a stand-still; at a stand. 2. Unshaken; unwavering; bent upon; set upon; unswerving; unflinching. Phr. Through thick and thin; coute qui coute ; game to the backbone. (Slang) . 3. Steadfast; staunch; persistent; persevering; undeviating. Woh apne irāde meṅ aṭal hai . 4. Proof against; steeled; firm; unbending; unyielding; inflexible; decided; resolved; resolute; strong; determined. Woh baṛā aṭal hai, kisī se muṅh nahīṅ phertā . 5. Stubborn; dogged; headstrong; inexorable. Phr. Per fas et nefas; foṛtiteṛ in re . Paṛe haiṅge aṭal ho, phūle phāle arne bhaiṅse se . Saudā. Their bellies are so full that they lie, like wild buffaloes, unable to move. 6. Unchangeable; immutable; constant; invariable; indefeasable; indissoluble; indelible; irreversible; inevitable; irrevocable; insusc…

def: adj. Strange, odd, singular, unusual; unpleasant, wearisome, dull; uneasy, restless, perturbed; — s.m. Strangeness, oddness, newness; unpleasantness, dullness, irksomeness; uneasiness, restlessness.
more: adj. au bad, lakhnā to see. 1. New; unused to; strange; queer. Makān aulū sā lagtā hai . 2. Unusual; unaccustomed; unwonted; singular. 3. Unpleasant; irksome; tedious; heavy. 4. Disquieted; restless; uneasy.
n. m. 1. Strangeness; newness. 2. Unpleasantness; irksomeness. 3. Uneasiness; restlessness.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُولُو (ʔulū).

def: s.m. Aid, help, assistance; — aider, helper; defender, protector, guard.
more: s. m. Aid, assistance, a defender, an aider.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Deep ( = gahrā); sunk.
s.f. Depth.
n. f. Depth ( gahrāī ).
adj. (f. -ī), With the face down; upset, upside down, overturned, inverted, topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, ruined; adverse (as fortune); crooked, distorted; — s.m. Blockhead, fool
more: adj. H. oṅ down, S. धा to place. 1. Inverted; inverse; upside down; topsy turvy; overturned; adverse. Auṅdhā nasīb, phūṭe karm . Prov. saying. Upturned my fate and cracked my pate. 2. Crooked; distorted; wrong; perverse. auṅdhī peshānī, yā khoprī , n. f. See ulṭī khoprī. auṅdhe muṅh dūdh pīnā , v. n. lit . To suck with the head down. To be babyish, childish, ignorant. Phr. Not to know a hawk from a handsaw. Tum to kuchh jānte hī nāhīṅ, auṅdhe muṅh dūd pīte ho! You know nothing, you're a sucking baby, taking milk with your head down. auṅdhe muṅh gir'nā , v. n. To fall on one's face, or belly. Auṅdhe muṅh, Shaitān kā dhakkā ! Prov. Fallen on your face, it's a shove from the devil! auṅdhe honā , v. n. To be tipsy or drunk; to get under the table. Ek hī piyālī meṅ ho gayā auṅdhā .

adv. Turned over; upside down; inside out; heels over head. Auṅdhā chale, auṅdho batlāve, auṅdho jag meṅ āë, Kahe Kabīr, auṅdhī kartūti sīdhā huā nā jāë . Kabīr. Crooked ways and crooked speech, and upside down brought forth, Thus saith Kabīr, all crooked deeds shall crooked be henceforth.

n. m. 1. A fool; an idiot; simpleton; blockhead. 2. ( Slang ). A catamite. Auṅdhā khāë lauṅdā . Prov. auṅdhe kā jan'ā , Wom. n. m. Son of an idiot; a great fool. (An abuse). auṅdhā leṭnā, auṅdhā sīdhā —, v. n. To lie on one's face or belly. Auṅdhā sīdhā na leṭ, koī kal be-kal ho-jāëgī . Wom.
adj. Upside down, overturned. اوندهابخت aundhā baḵẖt, s. m. Misfortune, adverse fortune. اوندهي پيشاني aundhī peshānī, adj. Shortsighted, unfortunate.
subs: v.n. To lie on the face
adj. Crooked-headed; ill-fated, unlucky, unfortunate; shortsighted
adv. On the face, with the face or head down; head-foremost, headlong
lit. 'To suck with the face down'; to act like a child, to be silly, ignorant, &c.

def: v.t. To turn upside down, turn over, upset, invert, overturn, reverse; to pour out (water), empty (a vessel); to hang down (the head)
more: v. a. 1. To turn over; turn upside down; invert. Is kūṅḍe ko auṅdhā do . 2. To pour out; empty. Kyoṅ pānī uṅdhā rahe ho?

def: v.n. To be overturned, to turn upside down, upset; to be poured out; to lower (as the sky).
more: v. a. To turn upside down, to reverse, invert, overturn, overset, spill.
v. n. To lower (as the sky).

def: v. a. ( Ṭhagī .) To eat.
adv. More or less.
adv. S. ऊन less, H. paun three quarters. From 25 to one per cent. less. At a loss. Aune paune bech-kar jamā palle bāṅdho. aune paune bech'nā , v. n. To sell at a loss.
adv. At whatever can be got, at a loss.
s.f. Silence, dumbness.
s.f. & adv. Roving about, rambling, sauntering.
s.m. = آنولا āṅʼolā, q.v.
v.t. To boil, &c. = auṭānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to boil; to boil, boil down; to heat.
s.f. = auṭāʼonī, q.v. s.f. An earthen vessel in which milk is boiled.
v.n. To be boiled, &c.
more: v. n. Same as اوٿنا auṭnā, q. v.

def: v. n. 1. To be boiled (any liquid). 2. To consume with rage or vexation. Tere lachchhanoṅ se maiṅ rāt din auṅṭūṅ hūṅ . Wom.
adj. And, also, for the rest, besides, again, moreover; but, yet, still; over, else; and lo!; — another, other, different; more, additional
more: adj. S. अपर Pr. avar another. 1. Different; other; not the same; foreign. Ham khūb samajhte haiṅ terī tarz-i-nigah ko, Hai qahr kī āṅkh aur, muhabbat kī nazar aur . Dā/?/g. 2. Only; no more; only that. Dunyā hai, aur matlab . Prov. Self and the world is all. 3. More; greater; worse. Saut par saut, aur jalāpā . Prov. Another and another co-wife yet, so burning coals on coals of fire are set.

conj. 1. And; else; besides; also; likewise. 2. But; however; yet; still. 3. Than (Correlative of 'no sooner'). Chiknā ghaṛā hai, būṅd paṛī aur ḍhal gaī . Naghat. A greasy jar, the drop slides off no sooner fallen.

n. m. 1. Any one else; some body else. Mujhe aur, na tujhe ṭhaur . Prov. For me no other, for you no whither. (After a quarrel, the wife to her husband —"I may not take another husband, and you could not be happy elsewhere.") 2. Others; other people. Aur ko mile purakh man-māne, Mujhe mile nādāne! Wom. Song. Lāonī . Nice husbands others to their mind have found, Me wretched to this senseless boy fast bound. aur irādah , n. m. 1. A different or lurking design. 2. A double entendre. Har chand havas ne to kiyā aur irādah, Par rah gaë kuchh soch-ke woh māre hayā ke . Madārī Lāl. Howe'er the lust of sense a further purpose had, Yet on reflection its fulfilment shame forbad. aur bhī . And; more; still; more; also; moreover. aur to aur , adv. Moreover; besides that; put the rest aside. Aur to aur, sage bāp kī to māntā hī nahīṅ . Wom. Others apart, he wont even mind his own father. aur to kyā , …

conj. And, also. adj. More, other, else. اورايک aur ek, Another, separate, distinct, else. اورکيا aur kyā, What else? certainly. اورنهين تو aur nahīṅ to, And if not then, and otherwise. اورهي aur hī, Quite different, extraordinary.
subs: Others, others besides; other kinds of
And again, and more, still more, also, moreover
Others apart, others being put aside
All other, all the others, the rest
One word more; — only hear! just listen!
Anyone else, any other, another, different ( = koʼī aur)
Anything else, some other, some or something more; something different ( = kućh aur)
What else? what more? what next? — certainly, assuredly, of course
And if not then; and otherwise
And this that; moreover, besides that.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀯𑀭 (avara), from Sanskrit अपर (ápara, “other, different”). Cognate with Punjabi ہوْر (hor).

def: s.m. pl. (of wird), Daily rehearsals (of the Qǒrʼān), commemorations, devotional exercises.
more: s. plur. (of ورد wird ) Daily rehearsals, commemorations, devotion.

def: s.m. pl. (of waraq), Leaves (of a tree, &c.); pages.
s.m. A throne; — places where goods are manufactured for sale ( = S. avaḍranga)
subs: lit. 'Ornament of the throne'; name of an emperor of Hindūstūn (who died in 1707)
s.f. A kind of cloth; — a kind of eruption (said to have broken out in the reign of Aurangzeb)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اورنگ (awrang).

def: s. m. A kind of silk so called.
n. m. 1. A kind of cloth. 2. A boil, or eruption said to have broken out in the reign of Aurangzeb.
more: s. m. A kind of cloth so called.

def: s.m.f. Legitimate, legitimately born; — a legitimate child (i.e. by a wife of the same tribe).
adv. All round, about, around; lengthwise and breadthwise; — adj. Level, even ( = ćauras, q.v.).
more: adv. All around. auras chauras , adj. Level; even; smooth.
s. m. (dakh.) 1. Environs, confines; circumference, all about or around: see اطراف at̤rāf. 2. Breadth and length. 3. Capacity (of a vessel: Harris ).

ety: for A. عورة, v.n. fr. عور 'to be blind,' &c.
def: s.f. (orig.) The private part or parts (so called because it is abominable to uncover or expose them); — (in Urdū) a woman; a wife
more: n. f. lit . naked. A female (استري). Aurat par hāth uṭhānā achchhā nahīṅ . Prov. Saying. It is not right to lift one's hand on a woman. aurāt tārak-ul-dunyā ; H. ārjāṅ, jatī . A nun. aurat kī zāt ; Hin. aurat-bānī ; Rus. baiyar-bānī ; Mah. mastūrāt , n. f. The female sex; womankind. aurat-i-hāmila ; H. garabhvantī istrī , A pregnant woman. aurat-i-zī-rutbah . G. G. A woman of rank. aurat kā bhagā le jānā . G. G. Abduction. aurat kā rāj , Woman's reign or supremacy.
(Heb. from Heb. To blind, to strip naked, or To be naked) s. f. 1. The pudenda of man or woman, that which is concealed through modesty. 2. (in Persian and Hindī) A woman, a wife.
ex: یہ ہم گنہ گار عورتیں ہیں جو اہل جبہ کی تمکنت سے نہ رعب کھائیں نہ جان بیچیں نہ سر جھکائیں نہ ہاتھ جوڑیں
ye ham gunahgār aurtẽ ha͠i jo ahl-e-jubbā kī tamaknat se na rob khāẽ na jān becẽ na sar jhukāẽ na hāth joṛẽ
It is we sinful women who are not awed by the grandeur of those who wear gowns, who don't sell our lives, who don't bow our heads, who don't fold our hands together
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَوْرَت (awrat), from Arabic عَوْرَة (ʕawra, “genitals; nudity”), where the shift to "woman" in early New Indo-Aryan happened because it was considered immodest to expose the female body in public. First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi عَورَت ('vrt)/औरत (aurata).

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻaurat), Private parts, pudenda; women (the com. signification in Urdū).
more: s. f. plur. (of عورت in Persian and Hindī) Women.

ety: ʻaurat, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Of or relating to woman; like a woman.
s.m. Any rite performed in honour of the dead; obsequies; funeral solemnities.
adj. Crooked, deformed, badly-formed, ugly; inverted, transposed; — s.m. Crookedness, deceit ( = fareb)
adj. Mad, crazy, &c. = bāʼolā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Mad, crazy, deranged, demented, insane.
s.m. Submarine fire.
s.m. Rice sown in April-May and growing luxuriantly during the rainy season.
E. & W. n. m. S. अश् to eat. Rice sown in Chait or Bai-sākh (Aug. Sep.) and growing rapidly through the rainy season.
s.m. Noble properties or attributes, praiseworthy qualifications, &c.
more: s. plur. (of وصف) Praises, qualities, endowments, descriptions. اوصاف حميده auṣāf-i-ḥamīda, adj. Praiseworthy qualifications, noble properties.

def: n. f. Rus. Illness (بيماری).
s.m. Calmness, coolness, self-possession, presence of mind, courage; sense, senses; sensation
more: n. m. from sān sharpness. Sensibility; sense ( havās ); presence of mind. Ausān se bāt karo. ausān uṛ jānā, khatā honā, yā jāte rahnā , v. n. To be stunned, stupefied, at a loss; to stand aghast; lose one's senses. Ausān khatā hue, aql chakkar meṅ āī . The senses at fault, the judgment in a whirl.
s. m. 1. Sensation, sense. 2. Courage. 3. Presence of mind.
subs: ausān jāte-rahnā, To lose (one's) presence of mind, to lose (one's) senses, to be stupefied, to stand aghast

def: adj. Middle, middling, intervening, intermediate; average; — s.m. (f.?), Middle, medium, mean, average interval; mediocrity
more: (from وسط) 1. adj. Middle, middling, intervening. 2. s. m. The middle, medium, mean, interval. 3. Mediocrity, moderation.

def: adj. Midmost, middling, moderate; pertaining to the average.
s.f. Care, solicitude, anxiety. (Also written अवसेर.)
s.f. Care, solicitude, anxiety. (Also written अवसेर.)
s.f. Medicine, medicament, drug, any herb, mineral, &c. used in medicine
s. f. A medicament, a drug, any herb, mineral, &c. used in medicine.
adj. Medicinal; — s.m. Ingredient of a medicine; materia medica.
s.f. Secretion or rheum of the eyes.
s.m. pl. (of waṣī), Executors, administrators of the divine will, or of the will of mortals, legatees; preceptors, guardians.
more: s. plur. (of وصي) Executors, administrators (of the divine will, or of the wills of mortals).

ety: aus˚ = Prk. ओवस(इ) or अववस(इ) = S. अपवस(ति), rt. अप+वस्
def: v.n. To become musty; sticky, &c. = ubasnā, q.v. v.n. To give forth a smell, become fetid; to turn; to smell offensive (meat or food); to rot, putrefy, become rancid or rank; to become mouldy, musty, or mildewed; to ferment; to become sticky or viscous; to become stale, or useless; to be weary, to be out of patience.
more: W. v. n. To become sticky. See ubasnā (1. 2.)
Mah. v. a. To knead the flour.
v. n. To become musty, to rot, to putrefy.

def: adj. Without consideration, thoughtless, heedless, unconcerned.
s.m. Defunct person ( = pret); spirit of a deceased person; one who dies without leaving issue; an unmarried man; stupid fellow, dolt, blockhead, fool ( = mūrkh).
more: Hin. Wom. ( Contemptuously ) ūṭ'ṛā, ūti'yā, ut'tā; Old H. aūt; Gaṛh autā , n. m. S. अपुत्र from अ not, पुत्र a son. 1. One who dies unmarried; a batchelor. 2. A stupid person; a blockhead; a fool. 1. Bāman kā ūt . 2. Bārā muṭṭhī kā ūt . 3 Chālīs-serā ūt. ūt-pit'tar , n. m. The spirits of the dead. Is ko kisī ūt pittar kī ho gaī hai . Hin. ūt-paṭāṅg . 1. Nonsense. See aṛaṅg taṛaṅg (1). 2. Harsh, hard, bitter words. Ūt patāṅg beloge tau achchhā na hogā . 3. Words for which it is difficult to find a rhyme. Maiṅ ne kyā is gazal ko sahl kiyā, Qāfye hī the is ke ūt paṭāṅg . Mīr.
(perhaps, for s. अपुत्र ) s. m. 1. One who dies without leaving issue. 2. An unmarried man. 3. (in Hindī phraseology) A stupid person, blockhead, dunce.

def: s.m. pl. (of watad), Pegs, pins; props; chiefs, nobles; certain four saints (regarded as the props of the faith).
more: s. plur. (of وتد) Pegs; nobles chiefs.

more: 1. verbal n. (from اوٿنا auṭnā ) Boiling, evaporation by boiling. 2. s. m. A knife used for cutting tobacco.

def: v. a. To boil; boil down.

ety: caus. of auṭnā or auṅṭnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To cause to boil; to boil, boil down; to heat.
more: v. a. (caus. of اوٿنا) To cause to boil, to make hot.

n. f. An earthen vessel for boiling milk.

s.m. Excellence, superiority.

def: v.t. To boil; to evaporate over a fire; — v.n. To boil; to be hot; to be consumed with rage or vexation. (See auṅṭnā.)
more: 1. v. a. To boil (as milk), to evaporate over a fire. 2. v. n. To consume with rage or vexation.

def: s.m. Anxiety, disquietude, perturbation, uneasiness, regret; ardent desire, longing, eagerness, impatience.
s.m. Anxiety, disquietude, perturbation, uneasiness, regret; ardent desire, longing, eagerness, impatience.

def: s.m. Seeking (God's) protection; fleeing to for refuge; refuge, asylum.

more: s. plur. (of وضع) Behaviour, manners, breeding, politeness, &c.

(pl. of وزر), s.m. sing. & plur. Tool, instrument; arms, tools, instruments, apparatus, &c. = ālāt, q.v.
more: n. m. Pl. of وزر Tools. See آلات.
s. plur. (of وزر) 1. Sins or offences (but in Hindī not used in this sense). 2. Arms, tools, apparatus. 3. The rigging and implements of a ship or boat. 4. Asylums, places of defence.

def: s.m. Potter's kiln; kiln; furnace.
more: n. m. A potter's kiln ( bhaṭṭī ). 1. Māṅ kā peṭ kumhār kā āvā, koī kālā, koī gorā re . Prov. The mother's womb is as a potter's kiln, Pots black and fair both issue from within. 2. Ek āve ke bartan haiṅ . Prov. Chips of the same block. 3. Kisī kā āvā bigṛe, inkā khadāne kā khadānā bigaṛ gayā . Prov. Some potters find their kilns go wrong; for him the clay-pit has gone wrong.
s. m. (dakh.) A furnace or receptacle for melting (glass), a kiln (for bricks, &c.: Harris ).

def: s.m. A kiln, &c. = āwā, q.v. A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)
A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)

prop. n. The province of Oude.
